current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: factorized_topk improved from its worst prototype but is still far from the K=128 anchor, and the current built-in families are not showing a path to the target. This round opens a new family designed to be safer than pure nonlinear encoders by keeping the baseline linear path intact and only learning a nonlinear correction on top. full

hybrid_topk is the first family to improve the K=128 full frontier, but the gain is still small relative to the aggressive quality target. This round keeps the architecture fixed and changes only the optimizer to probe whether the family is currently undertrained rather than intrinsically capped.

hybrid_topk is the first family to improve the K=128 full frontier, but the gain is still small relative to the aggressive quality target. This round keeps the architecture fixed and changes only the optimizer to probe whether the family is currently undertrained rather than intrinsically capped. full

action.hypothesis: The stabilized factorized_topk crash was caused by an implementation bug in the widened hidden-path projection, not by the architecture itself. Fix the hidden-to-latent projection and rerun the same K=128 factorized_topk stabilization pass to recover a valid quality signal.
action.summary: Round 5 produced a shape-mismatch crash inside factorized_topk after the widening edit, so the next move is a direct bug-fix rerun rather than abandoning the family. This keeps the hypothesis narrow: validate the intended wider gated factorized encoder before making any new architectural decision.
action.self_review: This is the correct response to the last round because the failure was a concrete code bug, not a training-quality outcome. The rerun is still only worth one more round; if the fixed version remains poor, this branch should be deprioritized.
notes_to_memory: Round 5 factorized_topk crashed due to a hidden-to-latent shape mismatch in the widened encoder path, so that result should not be treated as architectural evidence.
Round 6 fixes factorized_topk by adding an explicit hidden-to-latent projection and excluding the unused base encoder from optimization before re-running the same stabilization test.
next_hypotheses: If the fixed factorized_topk now yields a valid proxy improvement, keep incubating the family with quality-focused tuning.
If the fixed factorized_topk still performs poorly under normal runtime, stop spending rounds on current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder design.

action.hypothesis: A hybrid encoder that preserves the strong linear topk path and adds a gated nonlinear residual to latent logits can improve quality without discarding the baseline behavior that already works. Test a new hybrid_topk family at K=128 as a quality-first architecture probe.
action.summary: factorized_topk improved from its worst prototype but is still far from the K=128 anchor, and the current built-in families are not showing a path to the target. This round opens a new family designed to be safer than pure nonlinear encoders by keeping the baseline linear path intact and only learning a nonlinear correction on top.
action.self_review: This is a broader architecture move, but it is still a single coherent bet: combine the proven linear encoder with a nonlinear residual branch instead of replacing it. The new family adds compute, so even a promising quality signal would need later cost validation.
notes_to_memory: The fixed factorized_topk stabilization improved proxy FVU from 0.4431915953755379 to 0.3485000617802143 without runtime regression, but it remains far from the K=128 topk anchor.
Round 7 opens hybrid_topk, a new family that keeps the baseline linear topk encoder and adds a gated nonlinear residual to latent logits rather than replacing the linear path outright.
next_hypotheses: If hybrid_topk shows a clear proxy gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or nonlinear branch width before promotion.

If hybrid_topk is also flat or worse, pause family proliferation and reconsider whether quality gains are more likely to come from topk-mainline training dynamics than from encoder architecture alone.

action.hypothesis: hybrid_topk has now beaten the K=128 full baseline, so the next quality-first move is to stabilize the same family with an optimizer better suited to its smooth nonlinear residual branch. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam to see whether denser adaptive updates improve FVU beyond the current full frontier.
action.summary: hybrid_topk is the first family to improve the K=128 full frontier, but the gain is still small relative to the aggressive quality target. This round keeps the architecture fixed and changes only the optimizer to probe whether the family is currently undertrained rather than intrinsically capped.
action.self_review: This is a disciplined follow-up because the family finally showed real full-run promise and deserves one quality-focused stabilization pass before opening more architectures. Adam may not help, but it tests a concrete training-dynamics hypothesis without confounding the model design.
notes_to_memory: hybrid_topk is now the best full K=128 frontier at FVU 0.18129383772611618, narrowly beating the previous topk anchor 0.18532327562570572.
Round 8 keeps focus on hybrid_topk and tests whether Adam can better optimize its nonlinear residual branch than signum.
next_hypotheses: If hybrid_topk plus Adam improves proxy FVU, promote it to full as the new mainline family candidate.
If hybrid_topk plus Adam is flat or worse, keep the family but try a higher-capacity quality pass such as larger expansion_factor before considering cost reduction or lower K.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
next_steps: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.

residual_topk.design_hypothesis: A residual two-branch encoder may reduce FVU at K=128 by letting the second encoder pass target what the first branch misses, which is directly aligned with the reconstruction objective. Test residual_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as a quality-first architecture probe.
next_steps: If residual_topk shows proxy improvement, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If residual_topk is also flat or worse, try factorized_topk next rather than returning to smaller-K search.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The stabilized factorized_topk crash was caused by an implementation bug in the widened hidden-path projection, not by the architecture itself. Fix the hidden-to-latent projection and rerun the same K=128 factorized_topk stabilization pass to recover a valid quality signal.
next_steps: If the fixed factorized_topk now yields a valid proxy improvement, keep incubating the family with quality-focused tuning.
If the fixed factorized_topk still performs poorly under normal runtime, stop spending rounds on current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder design.

next_move_guidance: If hybrid_topk plus Adam improves proxy FVU, promote it to full as the new mainline family candidate.
If hybrid_topk plus Adam is flat or worse, keep the family but try a higher-capacity quality pass such as larger expansion_factor before considering cost reduction or lower K.
